statement: Never in the field of human conflict was so much owed by so many to so few.
author: Winston Churchill

statement: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
author: Winston Churchill

statement: In war there is no prize for the runner-up.
author: General Omar Bradley

statement: Ours is a world of nuclear giants and ethical infants. We know more about war than we know about peace, more about killing than we know about living.
author: General Omar Bradley

statement: The soldier above all others prays for peace, for it is the soldier who must suffer and bear the deepest wounds and scars of war.
author: General Douglas MacArthur

statement: They died hard, those savage men - like wounded wolves at bay. They were filthy, and they were lousy, and they stunk. And I loved them.
author: General Douglas MacArthur

statement: We must be prepared to make heroic sacrifices for the cause of peace that we make ungrudgingly for the cause of war. There is no task that is more important or closer to my heart.
author: Albert Einstein

statement: War is an ugly thing, but not the ugliest of things. The decayed and degraded state of moral and patriotic feeling which thinks that nothing is worth war is much worse. The person who has nothing for which he is willing to fight, nothing which is more important than his own personal safety, is a miserable creature, and has no chance of being free unless made or kept so by the exertions of better men than himself.
author: John Stuart Mill

statement: Future years will never know the seething hell and the black infernal background, the countless minor scenes and interiors of the secession war; and it is best they should not. The real war will never get in the books.
author: Walt Whitman

statement: There's no honorable way to kill, no gentle way to destroy. There is nothing good in war. Except its ending.
author: Abraham Lincoln

statement: The death of one man is a tragedy. The death of millions is a statistic.
author: Joseph Stalin

statement: Death solves all problems - no man, no problem.
author: Joseph Stalin

statement: In the Soviet army it takes more courage to retreat than advance.
author: Joseph Stalin

statement: It is foolish and wrong to mourn the men who died. Rather we should thank God that such men lived.
author: General George S. Patton

statement: Never think that war, no matter how necessary, nor how justified, is not a crime.
author: Ernest Hemingway

statement: Every man's life ends the same way. It is only the details of how he lived and how he died that distinguish one man from another.
author: Ernest Hemingway

statement: All wars are civil wars, because all men are brothers.
author: Francois Fenelon

statement: I have never advocated war except as a means of peace.
author: Ulysses S. Grant

statement: Older men declare war. But it is the youth that must fight and die.
author: Herbert Hoover

statement: Only the dead have seen the end of war.
author: Plato

statement: Death is nothing, but to live defeated and inglorious is to die daily.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: It is well that war is so terrible, or we should get too fond of it.
author: Robert E. Lee

statement: A soldier will fight long and hard for a bit of colored ribbon.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: He who fears being conquered is sure of defeat.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: You must not fight too often with one enemy, or you will teach him all your art of war.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: There's a graveyard in northern France where all the dead boys from D-Day are buried. The white crosses reach from one horizon to the other. I remember looking it over and thinking it was a forest of graves. But the rows were like this, dizzying, diagonal, perfectly straight, so after all it wasn't a forest but an orchard of graves. Nothing to do with nature, unless you count human nature.
author: Barbara Kingsolver

statement: If we don't end war, war will end us.
author: H. G. Wells

statement: From my rotting body, flowers shall grow and I am in them and that is eternity.
author: Edvard Munch

statement: He who has a thousand friends has not a friend to spare, And he who has one enemy will meet him everywhere.
author: Ali ibn-Abi-Talib

statement: For the Angel of Death spread his wings on the blast, And breathed in the face of the foe as he pass'd; And the eyes of the sleepers wax'd deadly and chill, And their hearts but once heaved, and for ever grew still!
author: George Gordon Byron, The Destruction of Sennacherib

statement: They wrote in the old days that it is sweet and fitting to die for one's country. But in modern war, there is nothing sweet nor fitting in your dying. You will die like a dog for no good reason.
author: Ernest Hemingway

statement: There is many a boy here today who looks on war as all glory, but, boys, it is all hell. You can bear this warning voice to generations yet to come. I look upon war with horror.
author: General William Tecumseh Sherman

statement: War is as much a punishment to the punisher as it is to the sufferer.
author: Thomas Jefferson

statement: War would end if the dead could return.
author: Stanley Baldwin

statement: When you have to kill a man it costs nothing to be polite.
author: Winston Churchill

statement: Battles are won by slaughter and maneuver. The greater the general, the more he contributes in maneuver, the less he demands in slaughter.
author: Winston Churchill

statement: History will be kind to me for I intend to write it.
author: Winston Churchill

statement: We shall defend our island, whatever the cost may be, we shall fight on the beaches, we shall fight on the landing grounds, we shall fight in the fields and in the streets, we shall fight in the hills; we shall never surrender.
author: Winston Churchill

statement: When you get to the end of your rope, tie a knot and hang on.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

statement: A hero is no braver than an ordinary man, but he is brave five minutes longer.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

statement: It is a big step to take another human life. It is not to be done lightly.
author: Lt. Col. Tim Collins

statement: Our greatest glory is not in never failing, but in rising up every time we fail.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

statement: The characteristic of a genuine heroism is its persistency. All men have wandering impulses, fits and starts of generosity. But when you have resolved to be great, abide by yourself, and do not weakly try to reconcile yourself with the world. The heroic cannot be the common, nor the common the heroic.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

statement: If the opposition disarms, well and good. If it refuses to disarm, we shall disarm it ourselves.
author: Joseph Stalin

statement: The object of war is not to die for your country but to make the other bastard die for his.
author: General George S. Patton

statement: Better to fight for something than live for nothing.
author: General George S. Patton

statement: Courage is fear holding on a minute longer.
author: General George S. Patton

statement: We happy few, we band of brothers/For he today that sheds his blood with me/Shall be my brother.
author: William Shakespeare,’’ King Henry V’’

statement: Cowards die many times before their deaths; The valiant never taste of death but once.
author: William Shakespeare, ‘’Julius Caesar’’

statement: Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: There are only two forces in the world, the sword and the spirit. In the long run the sword will always be conquered by the spirit.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: There will one day spring from the brain of science a machine or force so fearful in its potentialities, so absolutely terrifying, that even man, the fighter, who will dare torture and death in order to inflict torture and death, will be appalled, and so abandon war forever.
author: Thomas A. Edison

statement: There are no atheists in foxholes, this isn't an argument against atheism, it's an argument against foxholes.
author: James Morrow

statement: Live as brave men; and if fortune is adverse, front its blows with brave hearts.
author: Cicero

statement: Courage and perseverance have a magical talisman, before which difficulties disappear and obstacles vanish into air.
author: John Quincy Adams

statement: Courage is being scared to death - but saddling up anyway.
author: John Wayne

statement: Above all things, never be afraid. The enemy who forces you to retreat is himself afraid of you at that very moment.
author: Andre Maurois

statement: I have never made but one prayer to God, a very short one: 'O Lord, make my enemies ridiculous.' And God granted it.
author: Voltaire

statement: Safeguarding the rights of others is the most noble and beautiful end of a human being.
author: Kahlil Gibran, ‘’The Voice of the Poet’’

statement: He conquers who endures.
author: Persius

statement: It is better to die on your feet than to live on your knees!
author: Emiliano Zapata

statement: You know the real meaning of peace only if you have been through the war.
author: Kosovar

statement: Those who have long enjoyed such privileges as we enjoy forget in time that men have died to win them.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

statement: In war there is no substitute for victory.
author: General Douglas MacArthur

statement: War is a series of catastrophes which result in victory.
author: Georges Clemenceau

statement: In war, truth is the first casualty.
author: Aeschylus

statement: Incoming fire has the right of way.
author: Unknown

statement: Mankind must put an end to war, or war will put an end to mankind.
author: John F. Kennedy

statement: War does not determine who is right, only who is left.
author: Bertrand Russell

statement: A ship without Marines is like a garment without buttons.
author: Admiral David D. Porter, USN

statement: The press is our chief ideological weapon.
author: Nikita Khrushchev

statement: Whether you like it or not, history is on our side. We will bury you!
author: Nikita Khrushchev

statement: If the enemy is in range, so are you.
author: Infantry Journal

statement: Cost of a single Javelin Missile: $80,000
author: Unknown

statement: Cost of a single Tomahawk Cruise Missile: $900,000
author: Unknown

statement: Cost of a single F-117A Nighthawk: $122 Million
author: Unknown

statement: Cost of a single F-22 Raptor: $135 Million
author: Unknown

statement: Cost of a single AC-130U Gunship: $190 Million
author: Unknown

statement: Cost of a single B-2 Bomber: $2.2 Billion
author: Unknown

statement: So long as there are men, there will be wars.
author: Albert Einstein

statement: Aim towards the Enemy.
author: Instruction printed on US Rocket Launcher

statement: I think the human race needs to think about killing. How much evil must we do to do good?
author: Robert McNamara

statement: Any military commander who is honest will admit he makes mistakes in the application of military power.
author: Robert McNamara

statement: You can make a throne of bayonets, but you cant sit on it for long.
author: Boris Yeltsin

statement: The deadliest weapon in the world is a Marine and his rifle!
author: General John J. Pershing

statement: Concentrated power has always been the enemy of liberty.
author: Ronald Reagan

statement: Whoever stands by a just cause cannot possibly be called a terrorist.
author: Yassar Arafat

statement: Nothing in life is so exhilarating as to be shot at without result.
author: Winston Churchill

statement: War is delightful to those who have not yet experienced it.
author: Erasmus

statement: Friendly fire, isn't.
author: Unknown

statement: Diplomats are just as essential in starting a war as soldiers are for finishing it.
author: Will Rogers

statement: I think that technologies are morally neutral until we apply them. It's only when we use them for good or for evil that they become good or evil.
author: William Gibson

statement: All that is necessary for evil to succeed is for good men to do nothing.
author: Edmund Burke

statement: The commander in the field is always right and the rear echelon is wrong, unless proved otherwise.
author: Colin Powell

statement: Freedom is not free, but the U.S. Marine Corps will pay most of your share.
author: Ned Dolan

statement: I know not with what weapons World War III will be fought, but World War IV will be fought with sticks and stones.
author: Albert Einstein

statement: The truth of the matter is that you always know the right thing to do. The hard part is doing it.
author: Norman Schwarzkopf

statement: If you know the enemy and know yourself you need not fear the results of a hundred battles.
author: Sun Tzu

statement: Nearly all men can stand adversity, but if you want to test a man's character, give him power.
author: Abraham Lincoln

statement: If we can't persuade nations with comparable values of the merits of our cause, we'd better reexamine our reasoning.
author: Robert McNamara

statement: The tree of liberty must be refreshed from time to time with the blood of patriots and tyrants.
author: Thomas Jefferson

statement: If the wings are traveling faster than the fuselage, it's probably a helicopter, and therefore, unsafe.
author: Unknown

statement: Five second fuses only last three seconds.
author: Infantry Journal

statement: If your attack is going too well, you're walking into an ambush.
author: Infantry Journal

statement: No battle plan survives contact with the enemy.
author: Colin Powell

statement: When the pin is pulled, Mr. Grenade is not our friend.
author: U.S. Army Training Notice

statement: A man may die, nations may rise and fall, but an idea lives on.
author: John F. Kennedy

statement: A leader leads by example, not by force.
author: Sun Tzu

statement: If you can't remember, the claymore is pointed toward you.
author: Unknown

statement: There are only two kinds of people that understand Marines: Marines and the enemy. Everyone else has a secondhand opinion.
author: General William Thornson

statement: The more marines I have around, the better I like it.
author: General Clark, U.S. Army

statement: Never forget that your weapon was made by the lowest bidder.
author: Unknown

statement: Keep looking below surface appearances. Don't shrink from doing so just because you might not like what you find.
author: Colin Powell

statement: Try to look unimportant; they may be low on ammo.
author: Infantry Journal

statement: The world will not accept dictatorship or domination.
author: Mikhail Gorbachev

statement: Tyrants have always some slight shade of virtue; they support the laws before destroying them.
author: Voltaire

statement: Heroes may not be braver than anyone else. They're just brave five minutes longer.
author: Ronald Reagan

statement: In the end, it was luck. We were *this* close to nuclear war, and luck prevented it.
author: Robert McNamara

statement: Some people live an entire lifetime and wonder if they have ever made a difference in the world, but the Marines don't have that problem.
author: Ronald Reagan

statement: It is generally inadvisable to eject directly over the area you just bombed.
author: U.S. Air Force Marshal

statement: We sleep safely in our beds because rough men stand ready in the night to visit violence on those who would harm us.
author: George Orwell (Misattributed, was actually written by Richard Grenier[1])

statement: If at first you don't succeed, call an air strike.
author: Unknown

statement: Tracers work both ways.
author: U.S. Army Ordinance

statement: Teamwork is essential, it gives them other people to shoot at.
author: Unknown

statement: The real and lasting victories are those of peace, and not of war.
author: Ralph Waldo Emmerson

statement: We're in a world in which the possibility of terrorism, married up with technology, could make us very, very sorry we didn't act.
author: Condoleeza Rice

statement: All warfare is based on deception.
author: Sun Tzu

statement: The indefinite combination of human infallibility and nuclear weapons will lead to the destruction of nations.
author: Robert McNamara

statement: In war, you win or lose, live or die and the difference is just an eyelash.
author: General Douglas MacArthur

statement: You can't say civilization don't advance, for in every war, they kill you in a new way.
author: Will Rogers

statement: They'll be no learning period with nuclear weapons. Make one mistake and you're going to destroy nations.
author: Robert McNamara

statement: It doesn't take a hero to order men into battle. It takes a hero to be one of those men who goes into battle.
author: General Norman Schwarzkopf

statement: Any soldier worth his salt should be antiwar. And still, there are things worth fighting for.
author: General Norman Schwarzkopf

statement: It is fatal to enter any war without the will to win it.
author: General Douglas MacArthur

statement: Let your plans be as dark and impenetrable as night, and when you move, fall like a thunderbolt.
author: Sun Tzu

statement: Anyone, who truly wants to go to war, has truly never been there before!
author: Larry Reeves

statement: Whoever said the pen is mightier than the sword obviously never encountered automatic weapons.
author: General Douglas MacArthur

statement: Whoever does not miss the Soviet Union has no heart. Whoever wants it back has no brain.
author: Vladimir Putin

statement: My first wish is to see this plague of mankind, war, banished from the earth.
author: George Washington

statement: Cluster bombing from B-52s are very, very, accurate. The bombs are guaranteed to always hit the ground.
author: USAF Ammo Troop

statement: If a man has done his best, what else is there?
author: General George S. Patton

statement: The bursting radius of a hand-grenade is always one foot greater than your jumping range.
author: Unknown

statement: The tyrant always talks as if he's preserving the best interests of his people when he actually acts to undermine them.
author: Ramman Kenoun

statement: Every tyrant who has lived has believed in freedom - for himself.
author: Elbert Hubbard

statement: If our country is worth dying for in time of war let us resolve that it is truly worth living for in time of peace.
author: Hamilton Fish

statement: Nationalism is an infantile disease. It is the measles of mankind.
author: Albert Einstein

statement: We must not confuse dissent with disloyalty.
author: Edward R Murrow

statement: I will fight for my country, but I will not lie for her.
author: Zora Neale Hurston

statement: As we express our gratitude, we must never forget that the highest appreciation is not to utter words, but to live by them.
author: John F Kennedy

statement: An eye for an eye makes the whole world blind.
author: Gandhi

statement: Traditional nationalism cannot survive the fissioning of the atom. One world or none.
author: Stuart Chase

statement: If you want a symbolic gesture, don't burn the flag; wash it.
author: Norman Thomas

statement: The nation is divided, half patriots and half traitors, and no man can tell which from which.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Patriotism is your conviction that this country is superior to all others because you were born in it.
author: George Bernard Shaw

statement: If you're ashamed to stand by your colors, you had better seek another flag.
author: Anonymous

statement: Revenge, at first though sweet, Bitter ere long back on itself recoils.
author: John Milton

statement: A citizen of America will cross the ocean to fight for democracy, but won't cross the street to vote...
author: Bill Vaughan

statement: Patriotism is supporting your country all the time, and your government when it deserves it.
author: Mark Twain

statement: I love America more than any other country in this world; and, exactly for this reason, I insist on the right to criticize her perpetually.
author: James Baldwin

statement: ...dissent, rebellion, and all-around hell-raising remain the true duty of patriots.
author: Barbara Ehrenreich

statement: Principle is OK up to a certain point, but principle doesn't do any good if you lose.
author: Dick Cheney

statement: If an injury has to be done to a man it should be so severe that his vengeance need not be feared.
author: Machiavelli

statement: Before you embark on a journey of revenge, you should first dig two graves.
author: Confucius

statement: It is lamentable, that to be a good patriot one must become the enemy of the rest of mankind.
author: Voltaire

statement: Ask not what your country can do for you, but what you can do for your country.
author: John F Kennedy

statement: Revenge is profitable.
author: Edward Gibbon

statement: Patriotism ruins history.
author: Goethe

statement: I would not say that the future is necessarily less predictable than the past. I think the past was not predictable when it started.
author: Donald Rumsfeld

statement: I only regret that I have but one life to give for my country.
author: Nathan Hale

statement: Live well. It is the greatest revenge.
author: The Talmud

statement: We know where they are. They're in the area around Tikrit and Baghdad and east, west, south and north somewhat.
author: Donald Rumsfeld

statement: I was born an American; I will live an American; I shall die an American!
author: Daniel Webster

statement: Patriotism varies, from a noble devotion to a moral lunacy.
author: WR Inge

statement: It is easy to take liberty for granted when you have never had it taken from you.
author: Dick Cheney

statement: A man's feet must be planted in his country, but his eyes should survey the world.
author: George Santayana

statement: One good act of vengeance deserves another.
author: Jon Jefferson

statement: You cannot get ahead while you are getting even.
author: Dick Armey

statement: A nation reveals itself not only by the men it produces but also by the men it honors, the men it remembers.
author: John F. Kennedy

statement: Patriotism is an arbitrary veneration of real estate above principles.
author: George Jean Nathan

statement: Soldiers usually win the battles and generals get the credit for them
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: War is fear cloaked in courage.
author: General William C. Westmoreland

statement: In peace, sons bury their fathers. In war, fathers bury their sons.
author: Herodotus

statement: Don't get mad, get even.
author: John F. Kennedy

statement: A man who would not risk his life for something does not deserve to live.
author: Martin Luther King
